author: @jplaplante5622
text: Bidirection gears. I wonder if this could be used to function as atrium and ventrical of an artficial heart. Reducing the parts to do two jobs, into a single part. Prehaps this was already tried and I am uninformed. Inhale and exhale coulld also be utilized with this. In and out of a hydrolic system? Thank you for sparking my synapses.

author: @RickCarroll-j5n
text: So are all channels being shut down

author: @yingqiangli6026
text: I’ve been checking out your videos on and off for almost 10 years, and I’ve learned so much from them. It’s a shame you decided to stop making videos, but I totally understand. Hopefully one day you’ll have some new discoveries and ideas to share with us again! Farewell, my mentor!

replies.author: @TMGpuntNL
replies.text:
man, that hurts.

I hoped he would one day show some new stuff as well, but he never will again.

author: @paulhanlon1452
text: I love the Omnibus editions. Thank you Robert

replies.author: @TMGpuntNL
replies.text:
To see him reply to every comment on this channel hurts a lot
A bit more attention might have changed things.

I do not think it would have, because of the pain he was in....
But man, it hurts to see you loving this editions, thanking him, and him replying the way he did: very welcome mate....
And now..... a month later he will no longer reply.

I have seen every video he has ever made, and it breaks my heart that he will never ever make a new one. Never ever reply the way he did to a comment like yours.

author: @Tony-r8k
text: I love this content of gears, Mr. Smith! Thanks to you, I'm  learning that gears are even more fascinating than I realized. I think, if possible, it would be awesome for you to create a wall mounted escapement geared in a way to grind and contain beans for the coffee you like to drink. Just one good weighted pull a little patience  and fresh ground coffee ready to brew! Anyways, great stuff thanks for sharing!

replies.author: @TnTOmnibus
replies.text: Thank you for the kind words - a coffee grinder would be a great project!

author: @BrownBabyJesus
text: The hammer/pliers multi tool in the thumbnail is epic, I used to own one 😂

replies.author: @TnTOmnibus
replies.text: it was a gift from Ross who runs intelligent tinkering!
